programs.name: Delaware Medicaid for the Elderly
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical assistance to eligible low-income seniors and disabled individuals, covering services like doctor visits, hospital care, and long-term care. In Delaware, it is administered through the Division of Medicaid and Medical Assistance for those meeting income and asset limits.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals in Delaware.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers health services up to full Medicaid benefits for qualified applicants.[3]

programs.name: Diamond State HCBS Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services to allow seniors and disabled individuals to live at home instead of institutions. Services include personal care, homemaker assistance, and adult day care, administered by DSAAPD.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors 65+ or adults 21+ with disabilities at risk of nursing home placement.
programs.benefit_value: Personalized services based on assessed needs, no fixed dollar amount.[3]

programs.name: Delaware PACE
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly coordinates comprehensive medical and social services for frail seniors. It enables participants to live safely in the community with wraparound care including transportation and therapy.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible and living in PACE service areas.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage for all Medicare and Medicaid services plus extras.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and more; SLMB covers Part B; QI covers Part B for slightly higher incomes, administered via Medicaid.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ with Medicare and incomes/assets below specified limits (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Premium payments: Part B ~$185/month (2026 est.); full cost-sharing for QMB.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Food First/SNAP
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy food for low-income households. Delaware administers federally funded SNAP with state supplements for seniors.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income below 200% FPL, higher asset limits for elderly.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household.[3]

programs.name: Delaware LIHEAP
programs.what_it_does: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program offers help with heating/cooling bills and crisis aid. Annual grants and emergency fuel payments administered statewide.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+ at or below 150-200% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,000 per household annually depending on need.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency in low-income homes through insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs. Reduces utility costs and health risks for seniors.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters including seniors at or below 200% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Senior Medicare Patrol (SMP/SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare options, billing issues, and fraud prevention. SHIP counselors assist with enrollment and appeals.[3]
programs.who_its_for: All Delaware Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors 65+.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling and education sessions.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors through Area Agencies on Aging. Promotes nutrition and reduces isolation via volunteer visits.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ who are unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at low or no cost (suggested donation).[3]

programs.name: Delaware Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, and counseling to support family caregivers of seniors. Includes financial assistance for relief services.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults 60+ or 18+ with disabilities.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,000/year per caregiver.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid work training at community sites for unemployed low-income seniors. Includes job placement support and benefits.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed individuals 55+ with income ≤125% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay for 20 hours/week (~$7,500/year subsidized).[2][3]

programs.name: Delaware Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for issues like benefits, housing, and abuse via Delaware Legal Help Link and AAAs. Represents low-income seniors in court if needed.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal advice and representation (value $200+/hour).[3]

programs.name: Delaware Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities. Investigates complaints and ensures quality care rights.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities in Delaware and their families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy and complaint resolution services.[3]

programs.name: Delaware Prescription Assistance Program (DPAP)
programs.what_it_does: DPAP provides a prescription benefit of up to $3,000 per year for eligible seniors or disabled individuals with low income. Qualified individuals pay a minimum co-payment of $5 or 25% of the cost, whichever is greater, and must have Medicare Part D.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents who are seniors or disabled with low income and enrolled in Medicare Part D.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical assistance program specific to Delaware, offering up to $3,000 annual prescription coverage beyond federal Medicare benefits.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $3,000 per year in prescription benefits.

programs.name: Nursing Home Transition Program
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program identifies, informs, and assists nursing home residents who want to move to community-based settings. It offers individualized case management to support the transition.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible nursing home residents in Delaware, particularly seniors and adults with physical disabilities.
programs.whats_unique: Delaware-specific state-funded initiative led by DSAAPD to transition individuals from nursing homes to community living, not a standard federal program.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Individualized case management and transition support services.

programs.name: Wilmington Senior Tax Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: This program assists homeowners facing imminent foreclosure due to delinquent property taxes, sewer, or water bills. It provides grants to help seniors maintain their homes.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners in Wilmington, Delaware, aged 62 or older.
programs.whats_unique: Localized to Wilmington in Delaware, offering targeted property tax relief grants up to $3,000, distinct from statewide or federal programs.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Grants up to $3,000.

programs.name: SCAT – Senior Citizens Affordable Taxi
programs.what_it_does: SCAT provides a 50% discount on taxi fares for eligible participants. It helps seniors access transportation affordably.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Senior citizens and persons with disabilities in Delaware.
programs.whats_unique: Delaware state program offering subsidized taxi discounts specifically for seniors and disabled individuals.[4]
programs.benefit_value: 50% discount on taxi fares.

programs.name: Over-60 Tuition Benefit
programs.what_it_does: This program allows eligible Delaware residents to complete undergraduate or graduate degrees tuition-free at the University of Delaware. It supports part-time or full-time enrollment for lifelong learning.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents aged 60 or older admitted to UD degree programs.
programs.whats_unique: Unique to University of Delaware for state residents 60+, providing free tuition for degree programs, not available nationwide.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free tuition for undergraduate or graduate degree programs.

programs.name: Assistive Devices Program
programs.what_it_does: This DSAAPD program provides equipment like kitchen aids, bathroom devices, mobility tools, or communication aids to promote independence. Benefits must directly support independent action or communication.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Adult Delawareans with disabilities, including seniors, meeting financial and medical eligibility.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded through DSAAPD, offering targeted assistive devices for independence, specific to Delaware residents.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Wide range of assistive equipment (value varies by need).

programs.name: Home Modification Program
programs.what_it_does: DSAAPD funds modifications to permanent residences, such as wheelchair ramps, for adults with long-term disabilities. It supports safe and independent community living.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents 18+ with long-term physical disabilities, including seniors, meeting financial/medical criteria.
programs.whats_unique: Delaware state-funded program via DSAAPD for home adaptations, beyond federal offerings.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Funding for home modifications (e.g., ramps; value per project).
